title: Arrays
description: Master contiguous memory collections in C++ using traditional fixed-size arrays and modern std::array wrappers.
learningObjectives: Declare, initialize, and access elements in fixed-size arrays
Explain contiguous memory layout and how indexing offsets work
Contrast raw arrays with modern standard array wrappers (std::array)

sections.title: Contiguous Memory and Cache Performance
sections.content: Arrays store sequential elements of the same type in contiguous memory blocks. This layout is exceptionally friendly to the CPU cache: when you access index 0, the cache line pre-fetches index 1, 2, and 3, minimizing high-latency RAM fetches. Traditional raw arrays (`int arr[10]`) decay to raw pointers, losing size information. Modern C++ introduces `std::array<T, N>`, a zero-overhead wrapper containing size metadata and boundary checks.

sections.title: Raw Arrays vs std::array
